title: PHP-FPM Error Log Pattern Detection (segfault, SIGSEGV)
dataSources: Segfaults in workers compromise availability and may indicate exploit attempts or buggy extensions.
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; optional Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (`Splunk_TA_nix`); custom `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for `phpfpm:*` sourcetypes
spl:
index=web sourcetype="phpfpm:log" OR index=main sourcetype="phpfpm:log"
| regex _raw="(?i)(SIGSEGV|segmentation fault|zend_mm_heap corrupted|child exited on signal)"
| stats count by host, pool
| where count >= 1
description: Segfault and heap corruption messages in FPM logs indicate unstable extensions or malicious input. These events warrant immediate isolation.
value: Reduces prolonged partial outages from recycling crash loops.
implementation: Forward php-fpm error log; set `LINE_BREAKER` for `[pool` blocks. Page on first match; attach core dump policy.
visualization: Time series (pool utilization, queue, latency percentiles), single value alerts, top charts for scripts and methods.
grandmaExplanation: We watch your PHP worker pools so busy sites do not run out of hands to answer requests.
